purpose: Record the discussion context and proposed Harness flow changes for multi-model review.

complexity.reason: Changes role authority, dispatch execution, review-1 ownership, evidence anchoring, and validator assumptions.

human_intent.summary: After requirements and execution documents are frozen, the human operator will manually dispatch GLM backend and Kimi frontend terminals. Implementers should run through implementation, embedded pre-review, scoped fixes, and review-1 evidence without a model bookkeeper. The human then uses review-1 output to choose fix direction or dispatch review-2 to Codex or Claude.
key_constraints: Codex/GPT and Claude provider sessions do not execute model-dispatch commands.
A model bookkeeper role should be removed or reduced to document authoring only.
GLM owns backend-heavy work; Kimi owns frontend-heavy work.
Review-1 must have frozen evidence that later reviewers can verify.

draft2: reviews/kimi-for-coding-round2.md (early DRAFT-2; folded into rev b)
draft2c: reviews/codex-gpt5-draft2c.md (REWORK on rev c; pins folded into rev c2; codex re-confirmed ACCEPT on rev c2 — relayed verbally by user 2026-07-08 20:54, no raw artifact yet)
reviews_received.draft2c_round_outcome: CLOSED 2xACCEPT on DRAFT-2 rev c2 (glm52 ACCEPT + codex-gpt5 ACCEPT re-confirm). Spec review-complete; next = land §14 framework changes.

decision_log.rationale: Double-owner (GLM backend + Kimi frontend) stages should reach review-1 with zero extra human involvement; do not start with serial single-worktree. Recorder must be a deterministic script, not a model. Fold the single unavoidable integration/serialization point into the human's existing review-2 preparation via scripts/prepare-review-2.
Consensus of GPT + Opus 4.6, human-approved as trial-mode hard gates (not suggestions), captured in 05-harness-environment-snapshot.md.

hard_requirements.D1: task branch does not write top-level status.json; only task-local evidence snapshot under evidence/<task-id>/; integration status.json written solely by prepare-review-2
hard_requirements.D2: record-checkpoint commit scope = source/test + task-local evidence dir; must not commit top-level status.json/70-handoff.md/other-task files/top-level 60-test-output.txt
hard_requirements.D3: backend/frontend pathspec frozen pre-dispatch in status.json.tasks[].allowed_scope, checked by validate-stage.py --phase dispatch-ready
hard_requirements.D4: first trial forbids schema/API contract/shared fixture changes; forbidden shared paths are hard gates; else fall back to single-owner serial

decision_log.rationale: After 4 DRAFT-2 reviews (all REWORK, none BLOCKED, converging), user chose recommended options for the load-bearing forks; folded into DRAFT-2 rev c.

choices.A_checkpoint: sidecar — checkpoint.json never committed to task branch; prepare-review-2 reads it cross-worktree and commits on integration branch (no global fingerprint-exclusion change; task_head is a single commit; tip==head_sha)
choices.B_mode_flag: new independent flag independent_task_branch_mode.enabled (not parallel_mode.enabled); dispatch-ready gate re-implemented under it
choices.C_no_renames: keep canonical unchanged (no --no-renames, existing fingerprints preserved); pin the exact git -c diff.renames=true recompute invocation across all sites; add rename fixtures
choices.D_review1_files: validator task mode reads task-local evidence/<task>/30-review-1.md; drop top-level 30-review-1.md requirement for this mode
choices.E_allow_harness_change: product task branches cannot set allow_harness_change=true; Harness changes only via serial enablement stage

decision_log.rationale: draft2c confirmation round: glm52 ACCEPT (all F1-F12 closed, A-E correct); codex-gpt5 REWORK on two pre-script execution-timing pins, both overlapping glm P3 R1/R2. Folded into rev c2; no conceptual disagreement, no BLOCKED.

pins.codex_R1_and_glm_R1: record-checkpoint must validate the task worktree's uncommitted candidate then commit C_e then run tests against C_e (base..branch is empty pre-commit); §9 reordered, fixture #14
pins.codex_R2_and_glm_R2: prepare-review-2 pins sequential --no-ff merges M1->M2 then ingest commit M3=merge_head, assert rebind + full tests only after merge_head exists, atomic rollback via git reset --hard T0; §10-7, fixture #15
pins.glm_R3: pre-ingest worktree tamper bounded by anti-stale-tip fingerprint equality; verdict-text forgery is an accepted human-trust assumption (noted, not a gate)

decision_log.rationale: User approved landing §14. Implemented on branch stage/2026-07-independent-task-branch-mode; not merged to main until user acceptance.

landed.validator: scripts/validate-stage.py (itbm required-files mapping + dispatch-ready gate; additive, existing paths unchanged)

decision_log.dry_run: scripts/tests/itbm_dry_run.py — 13/13 assertions pass (positive ①②③④ + validator pre-review + fail-closed ⑤⑥⑦⑧⑪⑫/⑮⑯)
decision_log.spec_fix_from_dry_run: §5 branch topology corrected: task branches use a separate task/<stage-id>/<owner> namespace, NOT nested under stage/<id> (git cannot hold both refs). Discovered by the dry-run fixture.

review_requests.model_id_rule: Use the reviewing model or provider name as the filename, for example claude-fable5.md, claude-opus4.8.md, glm52.md, kimi-for-coding.md, grok-build.md, gemini3.1pro.md, deepseek-v4.md.
expected_review_focus: Whether removing the model bookkeeper preserves enough evidence discipline.
Whether GLM/Kimi can safely execute through review-1 without central model orchestration.
Whether frozen evidence generated by implementers is enough when review-1 verifies it.
What validator/schema/workflow changes are required before adopting this mode.
